reviews.review_title: Effective and fun - but needs true multi
reviews.review_description:
Been using this for a couple of weeks and have really enjoyed it so far. There is a great selection of workouts with various levels of intensity.
I found the vr coaches are actually quite encouraging and at times funny.
I already have noticed that I am feeling fitter which is great .
The only thing holding me back from 5* is the lack of multiplayer option. Yes, you can create a call sorry and then all sync the same workout but this is fiddly. The fact that you are in a room with avatars and it imports the names of your friends feels a bit misleading. 
Being able to set up a private room and add friends so you can all truly compete for top score would be a huge improvement and surely something that is possible from a developer perspective.
Do this and that is an easy 5*

Had this app for a couple of days and it’s one that I will now use everyday, love the workouts and certainly delivers.
By far the best workout app on the quest.

Update 
purchased 10th of January 2023 had fun but got sidetracked and my workout faded  then life took a turn and threw blood cancer at me in 2024 so I’m back doing workouts everyday 4 weeks 6 days so far and it’s definitely made an improvement to my Mobility Dan and Rachel are very cool characters with them I definitely push through and at 63 years young I am very happy with this fitness app.
Keep up the good work

I have a lot of good things to say about it, but there is one thing that ruins it all.

This app or whatever you want to call it, has 7 DLCs at this point in time. The DLCs are more complex and harder than the base workouts. So you go and buy them, then for one reason or another you don't have internet. Tough luck, because all DLCs you paid for, plus some other things and features are gone.

The DLCs have always online DRM. Why? Because they can. If I knew this I wouldn't have wasted my money.

Stay away.

I love Bodycombat workouts, but the Quest 3 version of the app is frustrating. I constantly get stuck in offline mode even though my headset is connected to a strong Wi-Fi network. My internet connection is fine on every other app, but Bodycombat refuses to go online. The app fails to connect to the internet, which locks me out of most features and classes. I've already tried reinstalling the app completely, but it didn't help at all — the problem persists. I had every intention of purchasing other apps from this developer, but now I completely refuse to pay them another cent. If they can't even make a core feature like internet connectivity work properly, I'm genuinely afraid that their other products will have the same issues. Until they patch the networking bugs, I can't recommend this app — and honestly, I'd warn others to be cautious with their entire catalogue.

developer_response.body:
We’re very sorry to hear about your experience and we completely understand your frustration. This kind of connectivity issue can happen for several different reasons related either to the headset configuration or to the play/network environment, and in many cases we’re able to help users solve it successfully.

Please contact us directly at support@odderslab.com and we’ll do our best to investigate your specific case and guide you through possible solutions. Also, if you were considering purchasing any additional content, please note that there is currently a temporary sale active.

reviews.review_description:
I am very disappointed with the Les Mills app on Meta Quest 3. The workout itself is actually excellent, smart, motivating, and very well designed, but the app/game is full of bugs and it completely ruins the experience.

Since I bought it, I have had repeated problems with freezing, loading issues, and the workout not working properly when I am ready to train. It is extremely frustrating because I prepare myself to exercise, put the headset on, get motivated, and then the app freezes or does not work as it should.

For a paid fitness app, this is not acceptable. People buy this to stay active and work out regularly, not to waste time restarting the app or dealing with technical problems. The concept is brilliant, but the performance is unreliable and disappointing.

Please fix these bugs urgently, especially for Meta Quest 3 users. Until this is resolved, I cannot recommend this app, because the workout may be good, but it is useless if it does not work properly when you need it.

developer_response.body:
Hi! We’re very sorry to hear about your experience and understand how frustrating this must be. The good news is that the bugs you describe have already been fixed in previous updates, so this situation sounds like it could be related to your specific device setup, game version, or another local issue affecting the app’s performance.

Please contact us at support@odderslab.com so we can investigate your case directly and help you get everything working properly again. We’ll do our best to assist you as quickly as possible.

reviews.review_description:
Les Mills Bodycombat is an excellent fitness game, but it has become completely unplayable after Meta’s v2.1 system update. Both controllers have ZERO vibration and haptic feedback. I can’t feel my punches, can’t tell when to retract, and can’t even finish the warm-up properly. This is not the game’s fault at all — Meta’s disastrous update ruined the core VR experience.

I have submitted feedback to Meta support but received no response at all.
Please do not follow their example — please value your customers and speak up for us.

developer_response.body:
Hi! Please make sure you update the app to version 2.3, which is the latest available version. It’s possible that a recent update from Meta has caused this issue. Unfortunately, system updates from Meta are outside of our control and can sometimes affect how the game behaves. 

That said, please contact us at support@odderslab.com with your username and a brief description of the issue so we can look into your case more closely and assist you. Thanks for your understanding!

reviews.review_title: Needs all advanced add on options
reviews.review_description: I love the coaches and the app but I am tired of doing the same workouts. I bought a few add ons and one had three advanced workouts, out of the 9 or 10 workouts, and the other had zero advanced workouts. Also, the music is terrible.

Don't listen to a majority of these reviews. Many talk about a broken point system or bots being in place of real people, the commentary here and there. Yes, I know it's an issue to many..but they also said they are addressing this. This game isn't about getting points or working out with bots anyway..it's about YOU. Despite all the issues, you're still punching fast..bobbing and weaving..doing kicks. In the comfort of your own home? Come on now guys, it could be A LOT worse. Now, put the headset back on... stop complaining + whining and put your dukes up. You have work to do! Cheers 🥂

I’ve been using this fitness app everyday it’s decent when matched with real work outs /runs but no real multiplayer and the dlc’s are glitchy hand tracking sometimes is hit or miss you gotta stay centered but over all good experience great coaches I wish there was real multiplayer and I think they quit support not many updates

reviews.review_title: Pulles you in and then fail
reviews.review_description:
I purchase after trail period and it was great until in stated to get serious in using the app. Movements we've beinft recognised, I couldn't forfill the moves requested the training which is stupid! To get any support is difficult as need to log via external means. 
I'm very sadden but grateful as about to upgrade to Oculus 3 and this as a main reason but now finding the app fails, limiting support has made it easy; don't upgrade. 
I'm grateful to find out now and share experiences. 

Really disappointed  as app hafmassive ben8fitd if just worked  and had high hopes for app!

developer_response.body:
Hi! We’re really sorry to hear that your experience has been like this. From what you describe, it’s likely that the issues you’re encountering can be resolved. If you contact us at support@odderslab.com, we’ll do our best to help you and look into your case in detail.

Also, please keep in mind that if you’re using a Meta Quest 2, it is an older device and may not deliver the same level of performance as a Meta Quest 3 or 3S. We’d be happy to assist you and improve your experience if you reach out.

reviews.review_description:
Yo! I have been using this as my main form of exercise/workout for the past 3 months. Doing a session every other day mostly and some weeks even every day. It is really good! I usually go for runs for my anxiety and depression but that can be hard in the winter months and when it's raining a lot. So I've just done this instead.
No lie, my family and friends have all commented recently how I look more in shape. Asking me if I've been running and I've said I've just done this for 10-20 minutes a day or so. I've been told I look more fit and like I've lost weight. Kinda bittersweet comments because I didn't realise I had that much weight to lose. Haha. Especially when my sister was honest and said I'd lost my gut. Like, hey, I didn't know I had one, but...thanks?

Now i must say that this hasn't fully helped with my running stamina, as i ran again recently and I struggled. But this is still great cardio and what is the best is that it works all of your muscles, especially ones that you forget about. It's basically shadow boxing, so I don't think it is adding muscle and strength exactly but it is an awesome muscle toner as it stretches everything. It finds good ways to work everything and in a fun way, even working the legs that aren't tracked in VR. Even though I hate the long squat sections and I'm hit or miss with the knee strikes...I see the great benefit of those. The interactivity is really accurate. I've only had a couple stutters, and that could be my fault. But the graphics are sleek and impressive, and the impact makes it feel like you're actually knocking through targets!
The music is really encouraging too and has a nice little variety. I got someone to try it and they said when the music kicked in it really got them in the zone.
The instructors are cool as well! Now it is scripted, but they're humans instead of AI, and there is enough variety in what they say. There's a few overlaps and repeats, but actually I just find myself joining in their quotes when they say it like a comfort thing..."or is it a grimace"..."yeah! Like the superheroes do!" Both instructors actually help in distinct ways, and they made me realise my form and they help encourage and just keep you going in friendly ways that aren't jarring. For me anyway.
Now I must say that there can be significant difficulty spikes between light intensity and medium intensity workouts, but I found it good to experiment and test myself a bit each time. I have mostly done light intensity still with decent results (see my gut). There aren't loads of different workouts for each tier but there are still plenty and what do you expect. There seems to be about 10 in each or so and I like to dabble around depending. That's more than enough. For real.
Especially as it is thankfully a single price game, with optional DLC. I hate subscription models. I understand them but I hate them. So this is brilliant as a one time buy with enough to keep you going. That alone works well for it! On top of graphics and immersion, scenery, music, helpful instructors, accuracy. All that. It works best when you really go for it and do things with form as best you can. I was terrible at first but I think I have improved my form over time.
People seem to be annoyed at bots comparing with you and such, but that doesn't bother me so much. Sometimes it's annoying and doesn't seem to match, but it's about your personal workout anyway.  I hate all things AI so i don't want any of that involved, but this isn't a big issue. It's just a placeholder to see your performance if you want.
All in all, this works wonders if you give it a chance. Try it out, get in to it and appreciate it for what it is, and you won't be disappointed!

Hi there, I've been using Les Mills for about 2 months now and I love doing it after work. I can feel my fitness improving steadily. And it's still fun and challenging. I'm following a 30-day Les Mills challenge with Dr. Tine G Viking Princess VR and it's going really well. I also like that there are no monthly subscription costs and you can just buy a DLC if you want. However, it is recommended to watch a tutorial on YouTube about the correct way to stand while punching and kicking. For me personally, it's a fun way to get back into working out, something I hadn't done for a very long time.

I’ve tried almost every fitness app on Quest, but Les Mills Body Combat easily beats them all. The workouts are super dynamic, motivating, and actually make you feel like you’re in a real class!

In just 6 months I lost 15 kg and built real muscle — way better results than I ever got from a year at the gym. 

Don’t waste time looking for other fitness apps — just go for Body Combat. Highly recommended!

The problem is "no internet connection" , what should i do? I've tried to re-install, but always the same problem. The app is in "offline mode". I've latest meta quest 3 updates, but still the same error(

developer_response.body:
*UPDATE: We have investigated the issue, and according to our database, it appears that you are using an outdated version of the game. This could be the cause of the problems you are experiencing.

Please contact us again when you can and try reinstalling the game. In your email, please also send us the game version you currently have installed so we can verify this theory.

--

Hello! We’re very sorry for the inconvenience. Could you please contact us via email (support@odderslab.com) and send us your username? Ideally, it would also be very helpful if you could provide a short video showing the loading screen and the error message.

This information will allow us to investigate the issue more thoroughly and find a solution as quickly as possible. Thank you for your patience and cooperation.

reviews.review_title: Is this app being supported anymore?
reviews.review_description: I gave this 1-star to maybe get someone to respond? I LOVE this app but it's been glitchy - very glitchy over the past few months and it's getting worse. Today I was stuck in a tutorial loop before a workout - actually every workout and the person I work out with kept losing her screen- we're in two different states (we call each other and start the w/o at the same time). My premium would workouts are gone - even though I downloaded them AGAIN last week. I no longer even have the option to download. I'm totally bummed because this is the best fitness app IMO. Anyone else having issues?

developer_response.body:
Hi! If you’d like a faster response, the best way to reach us is by emailing us at support@odderslab.com with your username and a brief description of the issue, this helps us assist you much more effectively than through reviews.

Regarding your case, we’re aware of the DLC issue you’re experiencing and are currently working on a patch to fix it as soon as possible. That said, please do reach out by email so we can also look into the other problems you mentioned and help you resolve them.

reviews.review_description: Love working out to this.Very worth it!
I love the app. They should also add strength training programs with and without weights using mixed reality, even if it's a separate purchase.

reviews.review_title: Actually Makes Me Want to Work Out
reviews.review_description: I’ve tried quite a few VR fitness games, but LES MILLS BODYCOMBAT is the only one that actually stuck. The visuals are polished, the workouts are well-structured, and the variety of movements keeps things fresh. I really like the optional trainer voice in the background — it feels like a real class, keeps you motivated, and you can easily toggle it off if you prefer quiet. It’s the only fitness title I genuinely look forward to playing. Sure, any routine can feel repetitive eventually, but the amount of content here is huge, especially with all the DLC (I own them all). If you want real, engaging VR workouts instead of just waving controllers around, this is it. Absolutely worth the price.

Absolutely love this app. I've got quite a few fitness apps but this is the one that really feels like I'm doing a tough workout and registers as such on my Garmin. The coaches are actually telling you what to do unlike some others where they're just saying stuff like 'yay you've got this" etc. My favourite workout app, hope they bring out more DLC
I purchased the premium pack last year. When I was in the program yesterday it asked me to purchase the premium content, which is now missing. What is going on with the app?  Please fix ASAP!

developer_response.body: Hi! We’re very sorry for the inconvenience. We’ve just released an update, and all you need to do is reinstall the app to recover all your content. Thank you for your patience and understanding!

reviews.review_description: When I first got this, I was really impressed, and I still am. I would really like it if the game had some customisation options. I get that for most people, you want to push them harder and longer with their training all the time to improve their fitness. For me, I am an older person who is very sedentary. I want something that I can do at home and have a consitent constant level of difficulty and length of time. I am not trying to be super fit, I just want to get some light exercise. It would be great if you added some options to our profile where we can set the preferred difficulty and maybe a maximum difficulty. Also, the preferred length of the workout and the maximum length. Then on the home screen everyday you could suggest random workouts that match the preferred setting, and maybe also a maximum workout if I am feeling really good and want to push myself. Currently it always suggests workouts that are more difficult than I want to do and longer in time. I then need to go into the workouts and pick from a very small selection. You have the ability to do this because most of the long workouts are just multiple workouts bundled together. Why not let us have them all separated and built for what we want to achieve?

Have been using this app as a New Year’s resolution to get in shape. It’s been three whole months so far and I’ve gotten some great results with two to three workouts a week. It’s fun and motivating and the time goes by really fast. The standard content is good still but I will likely pick up a DLC soon to expand the workout options. 
Results - increased definition in chest, abs, and legs plus much better cardio. 
Previously I had played “ring fit adventure” but found it tedious and obnoxious to get through, though effective. Les Mills is just straight to the punch and doesn’t get bogged down in fluff. (Pun intended)
Highly recommend for anyone interested in using be to get in shape. Five stars for sure.

This is the best boxing VR game I’ve tried! The programs are very professionally structured, and I especially liked that there are targets for leg workouts as well. It’s a lot of fun, and it actually motivated me to start working out at home—it showed me that exercise can be both effective and enjoyable without leaving the house.

To make it even better, I’d suggest adding an option to create custom workouts with option to add custom music. Looking forward to new training packs in the future!

reviews.review_title: Fantastic workout app for all ages
reviews.review_description: Purchased this app after looking for something with more of a lower body focus as many of the other apps primarily focus on boxing and wow I wasn’t disappointed. There are 3 intensity levels, I typically use intermediate and advanced and both levels work up a sweat. I love how tabeta training has also been incorporated, it really gets the heart rate up. I have saved about 6 workouts as favourites as they are more full body focused (loads of squatting) I find I can handle around 15 minutes of advanced before fatiguing and I’m a relatively fit person so that should tell you how intense it is. Lots of core work involved too and there is an option to turn off the trainers if you just want to workout to the music. Overall a very well thought out app, looking forward to further expansion packs as they become available

I’ve had this app for a while and I can’t praise it enough. The trainers are so  positive and upbeat it really doesn’t feel like work. I get the workout I need when i need it. I am so lazy normally but I can get up and do my workout in the living room and set myself up for the day. Love it

reviews.review_title: This is great and fun whilst working out
reviews.review_description: A really great way to get a sweat on! I like the competitive aspect when in one of the sessions. Is accessible for beginner to.advance, as I get used to VR and using a headset for a workout. Very enjoyable and helps when I can't get to the gym.

reviews.review_title: A great app but needs more
reviews.review_description:
I have been using VR for exercise for a few years now and it works, Les Mills body combat has been a great addition to my home workouts and I have enjoyed it a great deal. The system works well, it has minor glitches here and there, but I’m a gamer and this is not anything new. I feel engaged when I do a workout and it’s a very fun way to exercise. This review is more of a comment on potentially improving the app, I would encourage anyone who is like me to buy this. Especially those who struggle with motivation, get bored lifting weights and often hate doing a workout after a long day at work. This app is the perfect balance between having some fun, getting a little competitive with it and just enjoying some movement. 

I would love to see:
Customisable music for workouts, potentially adding music packs and songs that you can select for each segment of the workout.

Customised workouts where you can select what kind of moves you do so you can customise segments of workouts  and remove elements that you may not like as much.

Add in a rewards system for streaks and high scores to unlock customisable gear and change the colours and noises for when you hit targets etc
I'll give you five star if you erase "Knee" exercises. Or at least make it more easier. It always kill my combos and don't know why. So stressful.

developer_response.body:
Hi! Thanks for your feedback. We understand it can be frustrating, but we can’t remove a movement that many players enjoy. The “Knee” exercise is actually a fairly simple one, so it’s possible that something in the execution or tracking might be off.

If you’d like, feel free to contact us at support@odderslab.com and we’ll do our best to help you improve it and get those combos back on track.

reviews.review_description: It’s a fun,active, sometimes even challenging game! The only problem i have with this is that it does not register some hits. I like to see my accuracy, combos, points. It not registering is a big no for me.

developer_response.body: Hi! If you send us an email at support@odderslab.com, we may be able to help you improve this. Issues with hit registration can sometimes be related to headset positioning, space setup configuration, or other environmental factors. If you can include your username and, if possible, a short video of the issue or a workout, it will help us take a closer look and assist you more effectively. Thanks!

reviews.review_title: The best workout app I’ve tried
reviews.review_description: My wife sans I have been using Les Mills Body Combat for a few weeks now. Hand down the best workout for the Quest 3 I’ve tried. It provides a lot of different levels and it’s fun. Totally worth the price.
My game won’t start says I need a subscription and I bought everything??and no refund available please help??

reviews.review_title: I want to coach Chinese dubbing, and AI
reviews.review_description: The coach only has voice and no subtitles. English throughout the whole process. If you can't do Chinese dubbing or Chinese subtitles, it's okay. If it really doesn't work, AI dubbing is also okay. So lazy

reviews.review_title: Workouts are great. Q3 controller issue
reviews.review_description: 5 stars for the workout part of it. I actually used to teach Body Pump classes many years ago and less mills has stood the test of time. Body Combat for the vr is a great value as a non subscription workout app. Very affordable and lots of workouts, even if you never buy a DLC. The issue is that it has a real problem with glitching Q3 controllers. It seems to be triggered by moving the arms lower for certain punches but it has happened everywhere. I’m only getting it on the left side. Nothing fixes it. It doesn’t happen in any other game, so it’s absolutely a Body Combat issue. Look it up on Reddit and you’ll see people with the same issue. The developers really need to figure it out and fix it. For a game that values combos and beating your previous score, it’s ridiculous that people are penalized through no fault of their own. It absolutely takes you out of the flow of the game. Really hoping they put effort into fixing this.

developer_response.body: Hi! Thanks for your detailed feedback, and we’re sorry for the inconvenience. To help us properly investigate and resolve this, we’ll need a bit more information. Please contact us at support@odderslab.com and include your username, device model, and if possible a video showing the error—that would be extremely helpful for our team to identify the issue and work on a fix. Thank you!

reviews.review_description: Love the one off purchase model. Also this game/workout assistant is better than all the similar apps that are using a subscription model. Love this app!!
Thank you! This really helps with my workout since I dont have to subscribe to anything, I just wish that you’ll expand the advance workout stuffs. Thanks!

I'm writing this review to warn everyone about the new DLC(Never surrender DLC) for this game.The new scenes and content are incredibly lazy and low-effort. It feels rushed and completely lacking in creativity.I strongly advise against purchasing this latest  DLC. We deserve better quality! Also ,the developers should really bring back the old scenes that we all loved.

​I’ve been playing Les Mills Bodycombat VR, and it’s honestly a game-changer. The graphics are sharp, the music is high-energy, and the overall experience is incredibly fun. It’s the perfect solution for staying fit at home, especially when you can’t head out due to weather or other circumstances.

​What sets this apart for me is the coaching. Having real, professional trainers guide you through every move makes a huge difference—it feels much more personal and motivating than a cartoon avatar. It’s a serious, satisfying workout that definitely burns calories. Highly recommended!

reviews.review_title: Seasoned Les Mills fan, but new to VR
reviews.review_description: I’ve been a Les Mills fan for years—Body Pump, Body Combat, all of it. So I honestly didn’t expect much from the VR version of Les Mills Body Combat… but WOW, was I wrong! This game absolutely blew me away. It keeps me energized, motivated, and excited to come back for more. Totally worth every penny, especially with how much it’s helping me push my cardio and fitness goals to the next level. Also, not having a subscription was a huge plus.
I love this app! Could you please add an option to mute the music or play our own music? That would be amazing.
If this game supported multiplayer, it would be the ultimate experience. I'm curious why it hasn't been implemented yet, as adding multiplayer would surely increase the player base."

I truly loved this app, it was pretty obvious that when starting a session you are not training with real people from the get go.
But now the session starts with other peoples having millions of points after one punch (literally millions), and at the last round you can see the score dropping to your level 🤷‍♂️
Please consider fixing the game basic functionalities before pushing more DLC

developer_response.body:
Hello! We’re aware of this issue and are currently working on fixing this bug. We’re very sorry for the inconvenience it’s causing, and we’re prioritizing a solution to resolve it as soon as possible.

Thank you for your feedback and patience!

reviews.review_description: Get me to sweat and I love the feeling of community when I exercise with other people.

reviews.review_title: This is a game changer for me…..
reviews.review_description: I have a young pup who can’t be left too long on his own so getting a good gym workout is impossible. To do this from my own living room is so helpful. The AI instructors feel like you’re actually working with someone. Work up a real sweat and looking forward to seeing some actual results in the coming weeks/months.

reviews.review_title: I don't get all the hate
reviews.review_description:
I love Le Mills and I've bought all the add-in DLC. They're different and there's something to enjoy in each one of them. Focus mode is probably my favorite at the moment. 

This game has gotten me to work out more reliably in VR then its competitors. And I'm hoping it stays popular and gets more and more new content. I'd love to see something like leg sensors added. 

I wish there was a chat community for it but I'm too lazy to start it and be in charge of it. 🤣

developer_response.body:
We’re really glad to hear you’re enjoying the game and the DLCs — and that it’s helping you stay consistent with your workouts 💪

Good news: there is a community already! You can find us on Facebook and Discord — just search for “Les Mills XR Community” and you’re more than welcome to join. That’s where we share updates, new content info, and chat with other players.

Thanks a lot for the feedback and for being part of the community!

reviews.review_description:
A few days ago, all of the DLC that I purchased has been removed.  


\n UPDATE - This appears to have been fixed.

Thanks to the developers, whom I already thank for the excellent work done, but would it be possible to add a session specifically for warming up the hands, arms, legs, etc.? 5 to 10 minutes, to prepare all the muscles, especially for high-level training sessions.

I’ve had this app for over a year and all of a sudden, with no changes, I can’t connect with any low targets.
I tried restarting, shutting down the quest 3, it didn’t work. I tried uninstalling and reinstalling it didn’t work.
I tried unchecking body tracking, a different location, changing the floor height… I tried to see if any other apps have the issue, no issue.

nothing is working.. so frustrating

developer_response.body:
Hi! Sorry for the inconvenience — we’ve already notified our development team so they can review this as soon as possible. 

For now, please contact us at support@odderslab.com with your username and a short description of the issue, so we can properly track your case and get back to you more easily. Thank you for your patience, and we appreciate you reporting this.

reviews.review_description:
Came back after about a year and wow, this app went from great to kinda meh. 

First warmup: super laggy and basically unplayable. 
First workout: Bots on the leaderboard score 4x the points a player can score… very fun… NOT. In the end they were losing points and went from 1.7 million to 600k?? What is this? Do you guys not use or test your own app?

developer_response.body:
Hello! We’re really sorry for the inconvenience. There is a known scoring bug affecting this issue and we’re actively working on a fix. A patch will be released very soon.

We test the app regularly, but if it hasn’t been used for a long time, there may be performance issues related to the device. Please feel free to contact us at support@odderslab.com or join our Facebook or Discord communities to stay informed about the patch and get help resolving the issue. Thank you for your feedback and patience.

reviews.review_description:
This works but has the most awful music, especially the rap tracks. It's like theu asked ai to make music based on the worst stereotypes and the most dire rhymes. 

I would like a routine builder, not just so I can make my own workouts, but so I can avoid the awful soundtrack.

Love the game. Bought all the extra sessions. Have my wife, son and daughter in law playing it.
But, I am not as strong as when I first started 4 months ago. Now my shoulder hurts when I do too much punching above my shoulders. I have so many sessions, and many that I never tried as yet.
Why can't they tell us how many squats, knees, and various punches their are on each of these sessions.
THEY DO IT ON POWERBALL VR
It would be so helpful to game users.
Hi, I have purchased all the add-ons, but they are now locked so I can't play them. What do I need to do to update this?

Love it. It's far from boring, it motivates and you can do it in your own home. I've worked out for 1hr with AVG BPM of 150 yesterday. Running is not for a heavy guy like me yet, gym is a financial and time commitment...plus it's kind of boring. This is really perfect for some, Including me. It has sometimes tracking issues and whatnot was mentioned already, but the tool is as good as you can make it work for you and this is a very good tool for entertainment and workout.

If you are looking for a way to sweat without staring at a gym wall, Les Mills Body Combat VR is a must-have. It successfully bridges the gap between high-end fitness instruction and rhythm gaming. Here is why it stands out:
1. The Fun Factor
Exercise often feels like a chore, but this app manages to make cardio genuinely addictive. It gamifies the experience perfectly—you aren't just doing reps; you are smashing targets, dodging walls, and shattering obstacles. The satisfying visual effects when you land a punch make you feel like an action movie hero rather than someone doing a workout. Time flies when you are focusing on your high score rather than the clock.
2. Incredible Audio & Atmosphere
The sound design is a massive highlight. The soundtrack is high-energy and perfectly synced to the movements, driving you to punch harder and move faster. Beyond the music, the coaching is top-tier. You have actual expert instructors (Dan and Rach) in your ear, providing motivation and technical cues that sound natural and encouraging, rather than robotic or repetitive.
3. Serious Effectiveness
Don't let the "game" aspect fool you—this is a legitimate workout. The routines incorporate boxing strikes, knee kicks, squats, and slips that engage your entire body. Even a short 15-minute session can leave you drenched in sweat with your heart rate soaring. It is a fantastic tool for building endurance and burning calories efficiently.
Great app, I use it regularly to keep fit, the customer support is quick and efficient.

developer_response.body:
Hi! First of all, apologies for the delayed response. We’ll get back to you as soon as possible regarding your email.

In the meantime, please try reinstalling the app and then pressing the “Buy” button on the DLCs again. You should not be charged—this should simply unlock them permanently. If this doesn’t solve the issue, please write to us again and we’ll take a closer look. Thanks for your patience!

reviews.review_description: I think this app has a really cool vibe. When I train using it, I start to feel like I'm becoming cooler myself. Even if that's just a self-delusion, it feels great. It's awesome that it's a one-time purchase app, no subscriptions. Additional contents are sold as paid DLCs, so I can just buy DLCs I'm interested in.
The app itself is a wonderful workout tool, but a recent update has removed all of the paid DLC that I previously had access to.

developer_response.body: Hi! We’re really sorry for the inconvenience. Please contact us at support@odderslab.com so we can help restore your DLC and get you back to enjoying the app.

reviews.review_description:
This workout will work your entire body!
I love it! The point system helps me to keep pushing where if I was just working out I would give up. I absolutely love it! I would highly recommend

I dont care if there are any better apps, this app is perfect for me. It's very fun and makes me competitive against the people I go against, whether they are real or not. People are already telling me how they notice a change in my physique after only 4 days, and the amount I sweat in this subzero weather let's me know this thing really works. I'll take this over the gym any day!

This game is amazing, very addictive. It's like Beat Saber where you use your fists instead of lightsabers, but still with its own unique style. It has an MR (Mixed Reality) mode.Furthermore, this game is online, so you play the sessions in groups and feel motivated to compete. And the coaches' voices are very motivating during the sessions; it's quite fun.

I have tried many VR fitness apps and I have to say this is the closest to actually boxing and legit moves. They are close proximity, left and right hand friendly at all times, battle ropes, the most realistic knees and a good rhythm that is not too slow and not too fast. Gives you time to actually go through with proper movements and not just flale your arms around. I workout and I have fought in the past and this still kicks my butt! I always end up going back to that app!

Tried for the first time yesterday and went for the 20min holiday challenge to unlock the new gloves.  By the end I was sweating my butt off and was out of breath but made it. This will be my new go to fitness app. I hope they keep including unlockable things as a reward for working out. The reward was really motivating! UPDATE 1/12/26 I really wish there was a way to control the speed so I can practice the routines and not feel overwhelmed when I can barely breathe

I bought a 3S just for this game. At the start I felt it was exactly what I wanted. But as I play more, I realise that it is far too repetitive. It feels like any time they want a 'hard' section, it is just doing a flurry of jabs for ages. I'm so sick of long chains of jabs or jab/cross. Some levels are good fun as there is variety, but it doesn't feel like there's many. 

After the last update the splash screen can't be skipped. The scoring is even more pointless; others in the list get completely unrealistic, and impossible, scores then their scores go backwards. What is the point? Why can't the other scores go up as they did when actually being played. It removes any sense of competition.

Lastly, I turned off coaches. Yest every time I open the game, I'm forced to watch the splash screen, then a quip from the coach. Then I open an exercise, I get another unskippable quip from a coach. 

I don't want to touch the DLC as I fear it'll just be more of the same. I don't even notice the gloves or environment, useless added bits.

developer_response.body: Hi, We’re sorry the game feels repetitive. We consider it a title with lots of varied content, and each DLC is very different, featuring new environments and workouts. The scoring issue is being fixed, and we’d really appreciate it if you could email us at support@odderslab.com with your username so we can follow up — especially regarding the splash screen issue — and make sure everything works correctly as soon as possible.  Thank you for your feedback!

reviews.review_description:
Great app, good workouts and trainer commentary.
Annoying hum in main menu that can’t be turned off.
Unfortunately resorted to AI slop assets and didn't think to even quality check

developer_response.body: Hello! Could you please specify which asset you are referring to? We don’t use any AI-generated assets—everything is created by our team and Les Mills team.

reviews.review_description:
We have 3 profiles in our quest 3s: me as parent and my son whose profile is supervised and my wife also has one.

Les mills is the only app that always stuck on the initial Les mills screen before it would show the next Les mills logo and the odders logo that comes after both profiles not mine. I thought it is just the supervised profile but it stucks on my wife's as well.

The solution is to login to my profile, start les mills then quit, logout, login to the profile that actually wants to play and this time it works.

Could you please suggest a solution for this one? It's pretty annoying.

Otherwise it's great we all love it.
Always a good workout and great motivation. I even dropped my gym membership since this program is fun to stick with!

Les Mills Bodycombat on Meta Quest is the best workout game available on the platform. The trainers are absolutely top-tier. Especially the male coach, his energy and motivational speech are incredibly powerful. It really pushes you to give your best. It works. It genuinely motivates you to move and keep going.

The only thing I feel is missing is more variety in the moves. For example, I really love elbow strikes, but I’ve only found one workout that includes them. Maybe the newer DLCs have more of that — I’m not sure. I’ll probably end up buying them eventually.

I was directed to this app by a bunch of YouTubers who rated it number 1 in their books and I decided to give it a go. I am not disappointed. I really struggle going to the gym. In all honesty I hate the gym. I get anxious and bored after spending about 3 total minutes on a treadmill or any workout tool so when I started using this app, I found myself immersed and entertained the whole time. I totally love it! I’m documenting my 30day challenge on Tiktok because this time I know I’m sticking to it. I would also like to add that I’ve never sweat like this in my life. I had to get another set of silicon covers for my vr set. I’m also thinking of investing in a vent fan to help with the occasional fogging up. Thank you for creating such an awesome app! I love that the pay is affordable but saying this, I am also happy to pay towards any extra features and also to support this app. So ping me when you have something lined up!

Was great, but became a stutter fest over time and through updates, leading to constant and massive hit registration issues, rendering the scoring system basically unreliable, frustrating and useless. Played on standalone Meta Quest Pro. Will update my review if they fix their performance issues.

developer_response.body: Hi! Just a few days ago we released an update that fixes most of the issues you mention. We’d really like to take a closer look and help you get back to playing smoothly and normally. Please contact us at support@odderslab.com so we can assist you directly. We’re very sorry for the inconvenience, and thank you for your patience.

reviews.review_description:
Have only done around 3 workouts so far, but this app is awesome! Really glad that it's included in the Horizon+ membership. The music and commentary could have been annoying if they'd have gotten it wrong, but so far I'm really enjoying both. 

I guess the only improvements that I can think of would be to have the odd 'this is how you do your guard whilst throwing a punch at someone' guidance video outtake. This would help people who've never had any martial arts training to get into good habits. Though, as this is more of an exercise game, this isn't essential.

Another thing they could do is add an 'enable insult' mode to change the commentary slightly to things like, 'well done fatty, keep it up', or, 'you suck so bad that your nickname should be the vacuum', etc. A bit of gentle insulting can be motivating.

Oh, and if you're wondering what the game actually has you doing, then it's pretty much various punches (jab, cross, hook, uppercut), slamming your fists up, down or out to the side, squatting to avoid things coming at you and dodging left and right (by leaning). In one workout, there was also grabbing something and kneeing it. But you should just try it because it's lots of fun and is actually a really good workout, especially considering you're largely stood in one place.

It does offer a good workout routine but the trainers are annoying. I dont like the false reinforcement jargon. It's not helpful. It would be nice it they were more interactive if I miss a target or need to up my power or change my form based on what I do. More constructive feedback. I feel its a lot of wasted storage space just to listen to prerecorded puff talk.

I love this app. One of my best ever purchases.

My only gripe is, when my quest 3 glitches out (usually an hour in, and not your fault) it starts pausing/leaving game sessions without any external input and all my progress is lost.

Today I was forcibly kicked from a 20 minute session with 30 seconds to go and no calories/time spent were calculated.

It would be nice if, when a session is cut short whatever work has been done can be calculated.

This is one of the best workout game on the Quest. The coaches have good little tidbits. The online scores make me push myself but if I'm feeling the burn (which I usually am) that is usually enough motivation to keep me going to the end of a workout. The different locations are really cool, my one complaint would be I wish you could customize the background for each work out instead of workouts being attached to the separate backgrounds (though I wouldn't be surprised to see that change in an update... updates have been very regular! Thank you for that devs!)

Had the game for just over a week. First couple of days were fun and everything seemed to work well. 
The last few days I’ve noticed the boundaries pop in and out all the times. The competitors scores are like double what you get; even when you don’t miss a target. I’ve had a few misses on targets lately that the game didn’t pick up.
Overall this is a great game. Makes working out fun. Gets your breathing up and I can feel my muscles sore later in the day. Some of the glitches can get frustrating with those of us that are competitive. I highly recommend!

Got this game after Supernatural decided to shut down further development and man what a difference. Different moves and intensity really makes this feel like a complete workout. This has been great for rehab after dislocating my knee a few months ago. Love the non-subscription model with DLC too. Look forward to keeping on using it as an addition to my workout routines.

reviews.review_title: [Edited] It just doesn't work. At all.
reviews.review_description:
Update-2: Please allow users to change the color of targets in the settings. Currently, it is fixed as yellow-green for the right hand and pink-purple for the left hand. This is very confusing when simultaneously using other fitness apps, which typically use either an orange-blue scheme or a cyberpunk pink-magenta scheme (where pink is for the right hand, not the left, which is even more confusing).

Update: Everything is working now (probably after one of the updates).

The old review:
I am a new user of this particular app, but I have extensive experience using several other fitness apps. I read both positive and negative reviews, but decided to give this app a chance and purchase it. Unfortunately, I agree with the negative comments—at least the latest version has bugs.
I used this app for the first day and was generally satisfied. But then I had an unpleasant experience when the app froze my Meta Quest 3 and it rebooted. And then a new problem began — the app simply won't start, I just stare endlessly at the Loading screen, after which I have to simply turn off the app. At first, I was still able to start it sometimes (after several restarts), but now I can't do it at all. 
Wasted money and time.
Loved this game but now the Game doesn’t load anymore!!

developer_response.body: Hi! We’re very sorry for the inconvenience. This issue has been reported by a small number of users and our team is currently investigating it. Could you please email us at support@odderslab.com with your username and headset model so we can look into your case and resolve it as soon as possible? Thank you!

reviews.review_description:
Edit 2:
The basic tracking and hit registration is just very badly broken and infuriatingly unreliable. For your own sanity I'd suggest avoiding it. It's just not good enough. You can be on track to beat your score then it'll randomly decide "Yeah, nah mate" and you can see the fist glitch somewhere else and it decides you didn't hit the targets. Especially bad on downward strikes where you can see your hands move past your head after you've actually moved them. Have they actually tested this software at all? 

Plenty of routines say something like "basic boxing" or "upper body" and then half of the actual hard work is squats. If it has squats, say it has squats. I get it that squats are good but I want to make the choice for myself when I want to do them.

The voice overs are a big miss. Sure they should provide some encouragement but  they just bring me into a rage. Don't say stuff like "feel amazing", I'm out of breath and feel dreadful. Don't remind me that my clothes are sticking to my body, that's disgusting and offputting. I used to like Australians but now I never want to hear another one.

Edit:
It's incredibly infuriating how bad the registration can be on downward strikes. It will randomly decide it doesn't want to count them, sometimes just on one side. Annoying as anything.

I had a brief internet outage in the middle of one session and despite my connection coming back it wouldn't reconnect and register my workout, which is annoying.

developer_response.body:
Hello! We’re really sorry for the inconvenience and frustration this has caused, and we appreciate you taking the time to share such detailed feedback.

We’re actively working on fixing the scoring and hit-registration issues, and we hope to have this resolved soon. From what we can read, the main bug you’re experiencing appears to be related to scoring/registration, if you’re open to it, please feel free to contact us at support@odderslab.com. That would allow us to follow up directly, gather more details, and help improve your experience as much as possible.

reviews.review_description: Great game! Love the way it transforms your area into a home gym. You really get a sweat on.
It makes me sweat. It is good but i get tired of just the same two trainers.
